sources.label: Proactive Investors - PWR US$9.1M US government follow-on contract (Jan 2026, pre-window)
Business News Australia - PWR follow-on A$13.5M aerospace deal (pre-window)
Market Index - PWH quote and broker consensus

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the latest hard evidence (US$9.1M US government aerospace cooling follow-on) dates to January 2026 and is already in the score. Today's -1.8% drift and a modest +10.5% YTD leave the pre-discovery thermal asymmetry neither crowded nor newly validated, so the strict-screen rank holds.
verdicts.bull: Motorsport-grade liquid cooling and micro-matrix heat exchangers plus a deepening US aerospace/defense order book give PWR a credible repurposing path into data-center thermal before institutional AI buyers find this $0.6B ASX name.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis is intact but unconfirmed: no data-center design win disclosed yet, and the stock is drifting with no fresh catalysts since the January government contract, so residual alpha is unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Data-center cooling never materializes as a segment; PWR stays a niche motorsport/aerospace supplier exposed to F1 rule cycles and lumpy US fiscal-year defense funding, and the latency premium decays.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: First disclosed data-center or hyperscaler-adjacent thermal contract, or FY26 results (Aug 2026) showing aerospace/A&D revenue inflection.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Small-cap illiquidity plus customer concentration; a delay in US government full-rate production funding would undercut the only hard growth leg.
